codeReview.summary: The fix commit adds a contract-faithful VAL-SCORE-003 test with 20 progressively stacked high-value leads and updates scoring compression to 1-decimal output, which makes top-decile scores distinguishable at the scorer level (e.g., 99.7 vs 99.6). However, the implementation still does not fully resolve the prior blocking failure in end-to-end behavior because the pipeline/UI canonicalization layer coerces scores back to integers, collapsing those distinct top-decile scores to ties (99 and 99).

issues.description: The saturation fix is negated at the output boundary: serialize_lead_for_ui() casts score with int(...), so distinguishable fractional top-decile scores from _score_with_breakdown (e.g., 99.7 vs 99.6) are collapsed to identical integers (99 vs 99). This breaks the intended VAL-SCORE-003 distinguishability in generated leads.json/dashboard data and means the fix does not fully address the prior blocking issue in runtime outputs.

canonicalize_lead() also coerces incoming score to int(...), which can further discard fractional precision when reloading existing state from JSON/DB. This is not the primary blocker for the new test path but reinforces precision loss across pipeline round-trips.

summary: FAIL. The fix resolves the scorer-level top-decile tie condition and adds a contract-faithful 20-lead test, but precision is still truncated to integers in pipeline serialization, so runtime lead outputs can still collapse top-decile distinguishability.
